# DarkSlide v1.1.1

DarkSlide 1.1.1 is a focused reliability release for film-base re-analysis. It prevents crop-guided analysis from replacing a good automatic estimate with stale, darker, or otherwise weaker evidence, while keeping the workflow clear when no better reference can be found.

## Highlights

- Film-base re-analysis now examines pixels outside the current crop, avoiding synthetic crop edges that could be mistaken for clear film rebate.
- Re-analysis results are proposed before being committed, so the active estimate stays unchanged until a candidate passes safety checks and is accepted.
- Stale proposals are discarded when image settings change while analysis is running.
- Substantially darker candidates are rejected unless they bring a meaningful confidence improvement.
- Materially different candidates require confirmation before replacing the current film-base reference.
- A confident existing estimate is preserved when re-analysis finds no better candidate, with clearer guidance when manual sampling is the best next step.
- Worker caches are invalidated only after an estimate is actually applied, keeping previews and downstream analysis consistent.

## Why This Matters

A weak film-base reference can shift color balance and density across the entire conversion. These safeguards make re-analysis conservative by design: DarkSlide now prefers a known, confident estimate over an ambiguous replacement and protects longer-running analysis from racing with edits made in the meantime.

## Desktop Downloads

GitHub Actions builds and attaches installers for each supported desktop platform:

- macOS: universal Apple Silicon and Intel `.dmg`
- Windows: `.msi` and NSIS `.exe`
- Linux: `.deb` and `.AppImage`

## Known Notes

- macOS builds are not notarized. On first launch, macOS may require opening the app through System Settings > Privacy & Security > Open Anyway.
- Windows and Linux builds are unsigned.
- RAW support is available in the desktop app and remains an advanced workflow; JPEG, PNG, TIFF, and WebP are the stable browser-supported formats.

## Checks

This release includes expanded coverage for stale proposals, unsafe candidate rejection, confirmation handling, no-result preservation, and film-base application across the worker boundary. GitHub Actions builds the desktop installers on macOS, Windows, and Linux and retains them as workflow artifacts.
